title: Distributed Firewall Rule Hit Counts by Application Tier
exclusions: Does **not** score raw east-west **flow-log** economics the way **UC-18.2.1** does with **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** verdict streams alone—this UC needs **`hit_count`** statistics and tier labels. Does **not** replace **UC-18.2.6** day-over-day **`ratio_to_base`** anomaly math; pair siblings when debating retirement. Does **not** audit **Gateway Firewall** Edge statistics. Does **not** prove **`revision_id`** drift (**UC-18.2.7**)—use audit feeds only to explain counter resets.
evidence: Saved search **`uc_18_2_15_dfw_tier_daily`**, optional summary **`summary_nsx_dfw_tiers`**, Dashboard Studio **`ds_nsx_dfw_tier_hits`**, and the **`nsx_vm_tier.csv`** change log in Git.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A production **`db`** tier **`ALLOW`** rule tied to payroll shows **`hit_eod=0`** for 96h while **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** lists the VM powered on and peers show healthy **`day_delta`**—Splunk raises **`stale_allow_candidate`** and the dashboard highlights **`tier_share_pct`** collapse, prompting SegOps to Traceflow the path before auditors find the gap.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During a documented DFW publish, **`vmware:nsx:audit`** records **`security-policies`** PATCH events and **`hit_count`** resets; **`change_ticket`** references the publish so alert macros suppress **`day_delta`** drops for 12h and no stale-rule page fires post-window.

dataSources: `index=vmware` / `index=vmware_nsx` / `index=network_sec` — **`vmware:nsx:dfw_stats`** (Policy `.../rules/{rule-id}/statistics` when enabled, else legacy **`GET /api/v1/firewall/sections/{section-id}/rules/{rule-id}/stats`**) carrying **`hit_count`**, **`packet_count`**, **`byte_count`**, **`session_count`**, **`rule_id`**, **`section_name`**, **`applied_to`**, **`transport_node_id`**, **`category`**; **`vmware:nsx:dfw_rules`** nightly Policy rule catalog (**`rule_name`**, **`action`**, **`logging`**, **`profiles`**); **`vmware:nsx:policy`** realization JSON (**`revision_id`**, **`path`**); **`vmware:nsx:audit`** (**`request_uri`**, **`user_name`**, **`operation`**) for publish correlation; **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** via **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** for VM hostname and power state. Ingestion tied to **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)); optional **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for high-volume Manager feeds.
app: **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) for NSX parsing plus **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) for **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`**. Reference **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) when forwarding Manager **`vmware:nsx:audit`** syslog at scale.
spl:
`comment("UC-18.2.15 NSX DFW statistics by application tier; correlate validation with vmware:nsx:policy, vmware:nsx:audit, vmware:vsphere:vcenter")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw_stats" earliest=-14d@d latest=now
| eval rule_ref=trim(coalesce(rule_id, rule_uuid, rule_path, resource_path, ""))
| eval section_ref=trim(coalesce(section_id, dfw_section, firewall_section, ""))
| eval act=upper(trim(coalesce(action, dfw_action, firewall_action, "")))
| eval tn_hint=trim(coalesce(transport_node_id, tn_uuid, esxi_host_uuid, ""))
| eval hit_abs=tonumber(coalesce(hit_count, cumulative_hits, hit_total, "0"))
| eval byte_abs=tonumber(coalesce(byte_count, octets, bytes_seen, "0"))
| eval pkt_abs=tonumber(coalesce(packet_count, packets, pkt_total, "0"))
| eval sess_abs=tonumber(coalesce(session_count, active_sessions, "0"))
| eval domain_nm=lower(trim(coalesce(nsx_domain, manager_cluster, "default")))
| eval vm_key=trim(coalesce(protected_vm, source_vm_name, src_vm, guest_hostname, workload_name, vm_display, ""))
| lookup nsx_vm_tier.csv vm_name AS vm_key OUTPUTNEW app_tier AS tier_lkp
| eval tier_tag=lower(trim(coalesce(tier_lkp, nsx_env_tier, segment_tier_label, policy_tier_tag, "unmapped")))
| where len(rule_ref)>0
| bin _time span=1d aligntime=@d
| stats max(hit_abs) AS hit_eod max(byte_abs) AS byte_eod max(pkt_abs) AS pkt_eod max(sess_abs) AS sess_eod
        dc(eval(if(len(tn_hint)>0, tn_hint, null()))) AS tn_coverage
        values(section_ref) AS section_vals latest(act) AS action_latest latest(vm_key) AS sample_vm values(domain_nm) AS domain_vals
    BY _time tier_tag rule_ref
| join type=left rule_ref [
    search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw_rules" earliest=-2d@d latest=now
    | eval rule_ref=trim(coalesce(rule_id, rule_uuid, rule_path, resource_path, ""))
    | stats latest(rule_name) AS rule_name latest(section_name) AS section_name
            latest(applied_to) AS applied_to_doc latest(action) AS rule_intent
        BY rule_ref
]
| sort 0 tier_tag rule_ref _time
| streamstats window=1 current=f global=f BY tier_tag rule_ref last(hit_eod) AS prev_hit
| eval day_delta=if(isnotnull(prev_hit), max(hit_eod - prev_hit, 0), null())
| eventstats sum(hit_eod) AS tier_day_total BY _time tier_tag
| eval tier_share_pct=if(tier_day_total>0, round(100 * hit_eod / tier_day_total, 2), null())
| eval stale_allow_candidate=if(match(action_latest,"^(ALLOW|ACCEPT)$") AND hit_eod==0, 1, 0)
| where _time>=relative_time(now(), "-7d@d")
| sort tier_tag -hit_eod
| head 5000
| table _time tier_tag rule_ref rule_name section_name applied_to_doc rule_intent action_latest hit_eod day_delta byte_eod pkt_eod sess_eod tn_coverage tier_share_pct stale_allow_candidate sample_vm domain_vals section_vals
description: Rolls VMware NSX Distributed Firewall REST **`hit_count`** telemetry into **`app_tier`** buckets (**`web`**, **`app`**, **`db`**, **`dmz`**) using **`nsx_vm_tier.csv`** lookups, joins nightly **`vmware:nsx:dfw_rules`** metadata for friendly **`rule_name`** / **`applied_to`** context, and surfaces zero-hit **`ALLOW`** rows plus lopsided **`tier_share_pct`** concentration so operators know which segmentation seams actually carry production bytes versus forgotten permits.
value: Microsegmentation backlogs drown in thousands of **`ALLOW`** lines—without tiered counter truth, teams either freeze every rule during reviews or delete active paths. Aligning **`hit_eod`**, **`day_delta`**, and **`tier_tag`** gives network, platform, and application owners a shared scoreboard for what must stay, what can shrink, and which CMDB gaps block honest reporting, shrinking outage risk during policy trims.
implementation: Collect **`vmware:nsx:dfw_stats`** on 15–45 minute HTTPS polls, mirror **`vmware:nsx:dfw_rules`**, ship **`vmware:nsx:policy`** + **`vmware:nsx:audit`**, refresh **`nsx_vm_tier.csv`**, deploy the SPL as **`uc_18_2_15_dfw_tier_daily`** and wire dashboard **`ds_nsx_dfw_tier_hits`** with throttled alerts on zero-hit mandatory **`db`** **`ALLOW`** rows.
visualization: (1) Heatmap **`tier_tag`** × **`rule_name`** colored by **`hit_eod`**. (2) Timechart **`tier_share_pct`**. (3) Table **`stale_allow_candidate`** with **`applied_to_doc`**. (4) Overlay **`day_delta`** spark lines per tier. (5) Single-value **`pct_unmapped`** VMs for data-quality gates.
schema: CIM **`Network_Traffic`** normalizes future correlated flow fields (`src`, `dst`, `action`, `rule`). **`Change`** frames **`vmware:nsx:audit`** / policy publishes affecting counters. **`Alerts`** covers notable-ready threshold rows derived from **`stale_allow_candidate`** and **`tier_share_pct`** guards.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **`Network_Traffic`** once DFW flow feeds share the same indexes; acceleration on **`Change`** is optional and usually unnecessary for low-volume audit pulls—prefer well-bounded summaries (`summary_nsx_dfw_tiers`) for leadership dashboards.
knownFalsePositives:
**dfw_stat_transport_skew** — Per-transport-node vsip slices arrive minutes apart during vSAN latency or NSX appliance CPU spikes; `hit_eod` plateaus on one ESXi host while another still climbs, so tier totals look lopsided until pagination completes. Require `dc(transport_node_id)` near expected cluster breadth before paging segmentation SMEs; align suppressions to `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` storage alarms—not application bridges alone.

**tier_lookup_hostname_drift** — VMs renamed in vCenter `guest.net` while `nsx_vm_tier.csv` still lists retired `vm_key` strings; `tier_tag` falls back to `unmapped` and crowds the roll-up even though DFW counters are true. Refresh the CSV from CMDB plus nightly `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` inventory jobs, and keep a `prev_hostname_alias` column for ninety-day tails.

**policy_publish_counter_refresh** — `vmware:nsx:audit` shows `security-policies` PATCH bursts where VMware resets statistics pointers; `day_delta` dives but `vmware:nsx:policy` realization timestamps prove benign. Join `rule_ref` to audit `request_uri` and throttle twelve hours when `change_ticket` matches `dfw_policy_publish`.

**applied_to_microsegment_idle** — `ALLOW` rows scoped to `applied_to` groups covering dormant lab segments naturally read zero `hit_eod`; that is desired quiet, not proof the rule is orphaned in production. Cross-check whether `tier_tag` maps to `prod` classes inside `nsx_vm_tier.csv` before opening retirement tickets.

**cross_tier_shared_service_blur** — Jump `ALLOW` rows servicing DNS, NTP, or patch mirrors legitimately hit multiple workload tiers from one rule; `tier_tag` assignment from a single `vm_key` under-represents the fan-out. Model shared rows with `service_tier_multi` tags or split dashboards by `applied_to_doc` scope instead of blaming noisy `tier_share_pct` math.

**logging_profile_off_quiet** — Operators disable `logging` on hot `ALLOW` rows to save EPS while `vmware:nsx:dfw_stats` REST counters still tick; Splunk shows traffic without companion `vmware:nsx:dfw_rules` `logged=true` hints—never downgrade retention solely because flow logs hushed.

**emergency_section_baseline_hits** — `ETHERNET` / `EMERGENCY` category rows accumulate hits from hypervisor plumbing and should not be graded like `APPLICATION` micro-segment rows. Filter `section_name` with `category_rank<=2` into a sibling panel so tier KPIs stay comparable week over week.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) on hosts that parse **`vmware:nsx:dfw_stats`**, **`vmware:nsx:dfw_rules`**, **`vmware:nsx:policy`**, and `vmware:nsx:audit`. Add **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) wherever you need guest display names from **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** to keep `nsx_vm_tier.csv` honest.
• **REST credentials** — Service accounts require `security-policies` read plus access to legacy `GET /api/v1/firewall/sections/{section-id}/rules/{rule-id}/stats` when Policy statistics APIs are not yet published for every domain. Store OAuth2 refresh tokens or session cookies in Splunk credential stanzas—not clipboard snippets on shared jump boxes.
• **Networking** — Allow modular-input hosts to reach NSX Manager VIPs on TCP/443 without TLS MITM that breaks client certificate handshakes. Honor HTTP 429 Retry-After headers; add jitter across poll cycles when rule cardinality exceeds tens of thousands.
• **Indexes & RBAC** — Land feeds on `index=vmware`, `index=vmware_nsx`, or `index=network_sec` per data governance. Analyst roles need those indexes in `srchIndexesAllowed` without granting blanket admin.
• **Clock sync** — Keep ESXi transport nodes, NSX Manager clusters, CMDB jobs, and Splunk indexers inside ~500 ms skew so `_time`, API timestamps, and daily bins align with VMware UI columns.
• **Lookup source of truth** — Maintain `nsx_vm_tier.csv` with at least `vm_name` and `app_tier` (`web`, `app`, `db`, `dmz`, optional `shared_svc`). Refresh from CMDB or NSX effective tags after every segmentation change window.
• **Retention** — Keep ≥90 days of history on DFW statistics so seven-day rollups and thirty-day stale-candidate reviews remain reproducible for internal audits.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) **`vmware:nsx:dfw_stats`** — Poll `GET /policy/api/v1/.../rules/{rule-id}/statistics` when available; fall back to `GET /api/v1/firewall/sections/{section-id}/rules/{rule-id}/stats` for sections still managed by the MP API. Emit JSON with `hit_count`, `packet_count`, `byte_count`, `session_count`, `rule_id`, `section_name`, `applied_to`, `transport_node_id`, `category`, and `ingest_ts` every 15–45 minutes.
(2) **`vmware:nsx:dfw_rules`** — Snapshot Policy `.../security-policies/{policy}/rules` into Splunk so `rule_name`, `logging`, `action`, and `profiles` survive UI edits even when counters reset.
(3) **`vmware:nsx:policy`** — Capture `revision_id`, `path`, and realization summaries so you can explain hit-count cliffs that coincide with publishes rather than attacks.
(4) `vmware:nsx:audit` — Ingest Manager audit trails (`request_uri`, `user_name`, `client_ip`, `operation`) via syslog or HTTPS polling to correlate counter resets with authorized publishes.
(5) **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** — Schedule TA inventory inputs for authoritative VM hostnames, cluster membership, and power state—used to validate `vm_key` quality before blaming firewalls.
(6) `props.conf` — Set `KV_MODE=json` for statistics payloads, precise `TIME_PREFIX`, and `FIELDALIAS` stubs toward CIM `Network_Traffic` when you also ingest flow logs in sibling UCs.
(7) **`transforms.conf`** — Register `LOOKUP-nsx_vm_tier` pointed at `nsx_vm_tier.csv`; enable case-folding if CMDB casing is inconsistent.
(8) **Volume math** — Budget 4–9 KB per rule per poll including per-transport-node facets; multiply by site replicas before assigning index quota.

**Step 2 — Create the search**
Promote the SPL into macro **`uc_18_2_15_dfw_tier_base`** after index names stabilize.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.15 NSX DFW statistics by application tier; correlate validation with vmware:nsx:policy, vmware:nsx:audit, vmware:vsphere:vcenter")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw_stats" earliest=-14d@d latest=now
| eval rule_ref=trim(coalesce(rule_id, rule_uuid, rule_path, resource_path, ""))
| eval section_ref=trim(coalesce(section_id, dfw_section, firewall_section, ""))
| eval act=upper(trim(coalesce(action, dfw_action, firewall_action, "")))
| eval tn_hint=trim(coalesce(transport_node_id, tn_uuid, esxi_host_uuid, ""))
| eval hit_abs=tonumber(coalesce(hit_count, cumulative_hits, hit_total, "0"))
| eval byte_abs=tonumber(coalesce(byte_count, octets, bytes_seen, "0"))
| eval pkt_abs=tonumber(coalesce(packet_count, packets, pkt_total, "0"))
| eval sess_abs=tonumber(coalesce(session_count, active_sessions, "0"))
| eval domain_nm=lower(trim(coalesce(nsx_domain, manager_cluster, "default")))
| eval vm_key=trim(coalesce(protected_vm, source_vm_name, src_vm, guest_hostname, workload_name, vm_display, ""))
| lookup nsx_vm_tier.csv vm_name AS vm_key OUTPUTNEW app_tier AS tier_lkp
| eval tier_tag=lower(trim(coalesce(tier_lkp, nsx_env_tier, segment_tier_label, policy_tier_tag, "unmapped")))
| where len(rule_ref)>0
| bin _time span=1d aligntime=@d
| stats max(hit_abs) AS hit_eod max(byte_abs) AS byte_eod max(pkt_abs) AS pkt_eod max(sess_abs) AS sess_eod
        dc(eval(if(len(tn_hint)>0, tn_hint, null()))) AS tn_coverage
        values(section_ref) AS section_vals latest(act) AS action_latest latest(vm_key) AS sample_vm values(domain_nm) AS domain_vals
    BY _time tier_tag rule_ref
| join type=left rule_ref [
    search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw_rules" earliest=-2d@d latest=now
    | eval rule_ref=trim(coalesce(rule_id, rule_uuid, rule_path, resource_path, ""))
    | stats latest(rule_name) AS rule_name latest(section_name) AS section_name
            latest(applied_to) AS applied_to_doc latest(action) AS rule_intent
        BY rule_ref
]
| sort 0 tier_tag rule_ref _time
| streamstats window=1 current=f global=f BY tier_tag rule_ref last(hit_eod) AS prev_hit
| eval day_delta=if(isnotnull(prev_hit), max(hit_eod - prev_hit, 0), null())
| eventstats sum(hit_eod) AS tier_day_total BY _time tier_tag
| eval tier_share_pct=if(tier_day_total>0, round(100 * hit_eod / tier_day_total, 2), null())
| eval stale_allow_candidate=if(match(action_latest,"^(ALLOW|ACCEPT)$") AND hit_eod==0, 1, 0)
| where _time>=relative_time(now(), "-7d@d")
| sort tier_tag -hit_eod
| head 5000
| table _time tier_tag rule_ref rule_name section_name applied_to_doc rule_intent action_latest hit_eod day_delta byte_eod pkt_eod sess_eod tn_coverage tier_share_pct stale_allow_candidate sample_vm domain_vals section_vals
```

**Understanding this SPL**
• **Monotonic counters** — Treat API `hit_count` as cumulative until a publish or clone resets it; `day_delta` isolates day-over-day momentum for allow-list hygiene.
• **Tier enrichment** — `lookup` binds workloads to business tiers; the `unmapped` bucket is a first-class signal for CMDB debt, not noise to drop silently.
• **Catalog join** — The `vmware:nsx:dfw_rules` subsearch supplies `rule_name`, `section_name`, and `applied_to_doc` when stats events only carry numeric `rule_ref` values.
• **Coverage metric** — `tn_coverage` approximates how many ESXi transport nodes reported a given rule—useful when pagination leaves partial slices during storage latency.
• **Share percentage** — `tier_share_pct` highlights concentration risk when a single ALLOW row dominates an entire `db` cohort.
• **Stale hints** — `stale_allow_candidate` tags zero-hit ALLOW rows for human review; never retire rules from this flag alone without Traceflow and UC-18.2.6 baselines.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) **UI parity** — Compare NSX Manager Distributed Firewall Hit Count columns to Splunk `hit_eod` for matching UTC day buckets.
(2) **API replay** — Curl the statistics URL with the collector token; verify integers match Splunk at the same timestamp.
(3) **Audit pairing** — When `day_delta` collapses, locate a matching `vmware:nsx:audit` PATCH to `security-policies` before paging application teams.
(4) **Policy context** — Read `revision_id` from `vmware:nsx:policy` snapshots during the same window; benign publishes merit suppression, not incident bridges.
(5) **vCenter naming** — Verify `vm_key` against `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` inventory when `tier_tag` skews toward `unmapped`.
(6) **Lookup completeness** — `| inputlookup nsx_vm_tier.csv | stats dc(vm_name)` should track active VM counts; shortfalls invalidate `tier_share_pct` comparisons.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
(1) **Saved search** — Schedule **`uc_18_2_15_dfw_tier_daily`** (example cron `30 6 * * *`, `-7d` window) and optionally summarize into **`summary_nsx_dfw_tiers`**.
(2) **Role separation** — Restrict editing of macros to **`splunk_role_nsx_seg`**; grant app owners search filters on `tier_tag` only.
(3) **Change hooks** — Bind GitOps DFW merges to CSV refresh tasks whenever NSX tags remap tiers.
(4) **Data-quality alert** — Page when unmapped VM percentage exceeds 8% for 24h—usually stale inventory rather than firewall faults.
(5) **Capacity cadence** — Monthly, inspect `byte_eod` growth by `tier_tag` to plan Manager statistics load and Splunk license.

**Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot**
**Visualization guidance** — Dashboard Studio layout **`ds_nsx_dfw_tier_hits`**: Row A heatmap `tier_tag` × `rule_name` colored by `hit_eod` (log color scale when `web` dominates). Row B time-series `tier_share_pct` for ALLOW vs DROP intents using `action_latest`. Row C detail table for `stale_allow_candidate=1` including `applied_to_doc` and `sample_vm`. Row D small multiples of `day_delta` per `tier_tag`. Use brush-linked selections; avoid pie charts once `rule_ref` cardinality passes a few thousand.

**Alert design** — **Critical** when `tier_tag=db`, `rule_name` appears in `mandatory_allow_rules` lookup, `hit_eod=0` for 72h, and `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` shows the workload powered on—signals broken segmentation or shadow paths. **High** when `day_delta` exceeds five times the trailing seven-day median for the same `rule_ref` without a matching audit publish—possible scan or miswired jump box. **Medium** when `tier_share_pct>85` on one ALLOW row for 48h—candidate over-broad `applied_to_doc`. Throttle composite keys `tier_tag`, `rule_ref`, and `domain_nm` for 12 hours; route `db` pages to DBA bridges, `web` pages to frontend SRE.

**Troubleshooting**
• **Null hit_count post-upgrade** — Confirm vsip VIBs reloaded on ESXi; read `vmware:nsx:syslog` for DFW filter reload banners before changing Splunk `props.conf`.
• **Join cardinality blowups** — Duplicate `rule_ref` values across NSX domains require adding `domain_nm` to `stats` `by` clauses prior to opening VMware support cases.
• **Frozen day_delta** — Collector TLS or proxy failures—inspect `python.log` on modular-input hosts.
• **All tier_tag unmapped** — Wrong lookup path or blank `vm_key`; rerun `splunk btool` checks after TA upgrades.
• **Ethernet noise** — `ETHERNET` / `EMERGENCY` sections carry hypervisor chatter; split them into a dedicated panel so application-tier KPIs stay comparable.
• **Search auto-finalized** — Raise `subsearch_maxout` temporarily or query summary data for executive vistas.
• **Audit pipeline lag** — Compare `_time` vs `_indextime` on `vmware:nsx:audit` when reconciling sudden `day_delta` drops.

**Instrumentation checklist** — Validate parsed fields with `fieldsummary` on `vmware:nsx:dfw_stats` after TA upgrades—priority keys include `hit_count`, `packet_count`, `byte_count`, `session_count`, `rule_id`, `section_name`, `applied_to`, `transport_node_id`, `category`, `vm_key`, `tier_tag`, `rule_ref`, `domain_nm`, `day_delta`, `tier_share_pct`, `stale_allow_candidate`, `tn_coverage`, `applied_to_doc`, `rule_intent`, `sample_vm`, `section_vals`, `domain_vals`, `hit_eod`, `byte_eod`, `pkt_eod`, `sess_eod`, `action_latest`, `rule_name`, `section_ref`, `tn_hint`, `tier_day_total`, `tier_lkp`, `act`, and `bytes_seen`.

**Compact operator notes** — **Index union** spans `vmware`, `vmware_nsx`, and `network_sec`; **Bin** aligns daily audit columns at `@d`; **Streamstats** derives `prev_hit` per composite key; **Join** hydrates `dfw_rules` metadata; **Lookup** binds the tier CSV; **Eventstats** totals `tier_day_total`; **Head** caps interactive sessions; **Summary indexes** shrink DMA cost for monthly leadership reviews.

**Glossary** — **DFW**: kernel distributed firewall. **vsip**: ESXi dvfilter module owning counters. **Hit count**: cumulative rule matches returned by REST. **Applied to**: policy scope expression. **Rule ref**: stable policy or rule identifier. **Tier tag**: business label (`web`/`app`/`db`/`dmz`). **Transport node**: ESXi TN UUID. **Traceflow**: VMware path verification tool. **Summary index**: Splunk rolled-up metrics for fast dashboards.

**Runbook anchors** — Pair this UC with **`UC-18.2.6`** hit-rate baselines, **`UC-18.2.1`** flow-volume context, and **`Traceflow`** screenshots before closing **stale-rule** tickets; archive **`ds_nsx_dfw_tier_hits`** PNGs beside **`CHG`** records for auditor requests.
grandmaExplanation: We tally kitchen passes still swiped at the bakery, grill, and cold vault every week—when one chit stays silent while neighbors buzz, an outdated permit can retire without starving a real dinner rush.
hardware: VMware NSX Distributed Firewall enforces on ESXi transport nodes (Dell PowerEdge R650/R750, HPE ProLiant DL360/DL380, Cisco UCS C-Series, Lenovo ThinkSystem) with NSX Manager clusters fronting Policy API services; statistics aggregation spans per-host vsip dataplane contexts.
